channel.title: Home on Memo
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Memo

item.title: Public Key Cryptography
item.description: Type of keys # RSA https://ocw.kyoto-u.ac.jp/ja/graduate-school-of-informatics-jp/06/video02/08 http://herb.h.kobe-u.ac.jp/RSA.html https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8017 DSA https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6605 ECDSA https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6979 EdDSA https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8032 SSH key # Comparing SSH Keys - RSA, DSA, ECDSA, or EdDSA? SSHの公開鍵暗号には「RSA」「DSA」「ECDSA」「EdDSA」のどれを使えばよいのか？ Key generation # RSA: ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 ECDSA: ssh-keygen -t ecdsa -b 521 EdDSA: ssh-keygen -t ed25519

item.title: Web Development
item.description: Use Gist # Copy script tag from up right of the Gist. Paste script tag in the body. Use prism.js # prism.js is a syntax highlighter written in js. Add the following code in the head. <link href='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.17.1/themes/prism-twilight.min.css' rel='stylesheet'/> <link href='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.17.1/plugins/line-numbers/prism-line-numbers.min.css' rel='stylesheet'/> Additionally, insert following code in the body. <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.17.1/prism.min.js'/> <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.17.1/plugins/autoloader/prism-autoloader.min.js'/> <script src='https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/prism/1.17.1/plugins/line-numbers/prism-line-numbers.min.js'/> Example: <pre class="line-numbers"><code class="language-c">#include &lt;stdio.h&gt; int main(){ printf("Hello, World!

item.title: Install TeX Live
item.description: System # OS: Arch, Ubuntu Install commands # Install texlive and language package: sudo pacman -S texlive-most texlive-langjapanese sudo apt install texlive-full Downloads jlisting.sty from https://osdn.net/projects/mytexpert/downloads/26068/jlisting.sty.bz2/ and decompress: bzip2 -dk jlisting.sty Add jlisting.sty: sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/share/texmf/tex/latex/jlisting sudo mv jlisting.sty /usr/local/share/texmf/tex/latex/jlisting/ cd /usr/local/share/texmf/tex/latex/ sudo mktexlsr

item.title: Install Rust
item.description: Installation # Access to Install Rust. You will get command below, if you use linux or Mac. curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh The command above create /home/username/.cargo/ and you can use rust by adding path of $HOME/.cargo/bin to $PATH. rustup # Update: rustup update Update rustup: rustup self update Uninstall: rustup self uninstall cargo # Create new project (executable): cargo new dir_name --bin Create new library: cargo new dir_name --lib By default, cargo add .

item.title: Install Android x86
item.description: System # Host: Linux KVM Guest: Android x86, Lineage OS Installation # Download *.iso from https://www.android-x86.org/ KVM config: Auto install: Android emulator: NoxPlayer

item.title: Initial Settings for Ubuntu
item.description: Systems # Ubuntu 18.04 LTS (minimal install) Ubuntu 20.04 LTS (minimal install) Settings for Ubuntu 18.04 on VirtualBox # wget https://github.com/Forest0923/memo/blob/master/scripts/settings_ubuntu_18_20.sh chmod 755 settings_ubuntu_18_20.sh sudo ./settings_ubuntu_18_20.sh Initial settings # Update and upgrade # At first, upgrade apt with following commands. sudo apt update sudo apt upgrade Change language of home directory # English name is easier to operate on the command line than Japanese name.

item.title: Install Arch Linux with BTRFS (and Backup with Timeshift)
item.description: System # CPU: Intel UEFI Boot Commands # Keymap # Change keymap: loadkeys jp106 Time # Timedatectl: timedatectl set-ntp true Mirrorlist # Optimize mirror list: pacman -Syy pacman -S reflector reflector -c Japan --sort rate -a 6 --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist Disk Partitioning # Disk partitioning: gdisk /dev/vda /dev/vda1: EFI system (200M) /dev/vda2: Linux filesystem Format partitions: mkfs.

item.title: Install KVM
item.description: Commands # Install: # Arch sudo pacman -S virt-manager qemu qemu-arch-extra ovmf vde2 \ ebtables dnsmasq bridge-utils openbsd-netcat # Ubuntu server sudo apt install qemu-kvm libvirt-daemon-system libvirt-clients bridge-utils virtinst Systemd: sudo systemctl enable libvirtd.service sudo systemctl start libvirtd.service Configuring bridge: sudo vim br10.xml <network> <name>br10</name> <forward mode='nat'> <nat> <port start='1024' end='65535'/> </nat> </forward> <bridge name='br10' stp='on' delay='0'/> <ip address='192.168.30.1' netmask='255.255.255.0'> <dhcp> <range start='192.

item.title: File Compression and Decompression (tar, zip, lzh, bz2)
item.description: Usage # Extension Compression Decompression zip zip -r output.zip dir unzip input.zip tar tar -cvf output.tar dir tar -xvf input.tar tar.gz tar -acvf output.tar.gz dir tar -xvf input.tar.gz tar.bz2 tar -acvf output.tar.bz2 dir tar -xvf input.tar.bz2 tar.xz tar -acvf output.tar.xz dir tar -xvf input.tar.xz lzh lha -c output.lzh dir lha -x input.lzh bz2 bzip2 -z input.

item.title: Install Kernel
item.description: System # OS: Ubuntu 18.04 (64bit) Current kernel: 5.3.0-42-generic Compile # Change directory to /usr/src. Get kernel source code with the following command (ver.5.4.1). wget https://mirrors.edge.kernel.org/pub/linux/kernel/v5.x/linux-5.4.1.tar.gz Decompress with tar. tar -xvf linux-5.4.1.tar.gz Change directory to linux-5.4.1. Before compiling you have to install some packages via apt. Run the following command. sudo apt install flex bison libssl-dev libelf-dev Make .config with the following command.

item.title: GPG Key
item.description: GPG (GNU Privacy Key) List keys Add GPG key Remove GPG key Key servers List keys # List public gpg keys: gpg --list-keys List secret gpg keys: gpg --list-secret-keys Add GPG key # Receive keys: gpg --keyserver keys.gnupg.net --recv-keys [key id] Remove GPG key # Remove keys: gpg --delete-keys [key id] Key servers # keys.openpgp.org keys.

item.title: Cheat Sheet for LaTeX
item.description: Expression # Expression Numbers # If you need expression numbers: \begin{eqnarray} y=\alpha x + \beta \end{eqnarray} If you don’t need expression numbers: \[ y=\alpha x + \beta \] Align Equivalents # \[ \cos 2 \alpha &=& \cos^2 \alpha - \sin^2 \alpha\\ &=& 2 \cos^2 \alpha - 1\\ &=& 1 - 2 \sin^2 \alpha \] % or \begin{eqnarray} \cos 2 \alpha &=& \cos^2 \alpha - \sin^2 \alpha\\ &=& 2 \cos^2 \alpha - 1\\ &=& 1 - 2 \sin^2 \alpha \end{eqnarray} Simultaneous Equations # \begin{eqnarray} \begin{cases} \left(x_1 - 2\right)^2 + x_2^2 + x_3^2 = 1\\ x_1 + x_3 = 3 \end{cases} \end{eqnarray} % or \begin{numcases} {} \left(x_1 - 2\right)^2 + x_2^2 + x_3^2 = 1\\ x_1 + x_3 = 3 \end{numcases} Matrix # \begin{eqnarray} \left( \begin{array}{ccccc} a_{11} & \cdots & a_{1i} & \cdots & a_{1n}\\ \vdots & \ddots & & & \vdots \\ a_{i1} & & a_{ii} & & a_{in} \\ \vdots & & & \ddots & \vdots \\ a_{n1} & \cdots & a_{ni} & \cdots & a_{nn} \end{array} \right) \end{eqnarray} Source Code # minted \begin{minted}[linenos, breaklines, frame=lines]{c} #include <stdio.

item.title: Inline Assembly in Rust
item.description: Inline assembler in rust # llvm_asm!() # Sample code: #![feature(llvm_asm)] fn main() { let i: u64 = 3; let j: u64 = 5; let o: u64; unsafe { llvm_asm!("mov $1, $0; add $2, $0;" : "=r"(o) : "{bx}"(i), "{cx}"(j)); } println!("{}", o); } Output: % rustup run nightly cargo run Compiling asm_test v0.1.0 (/home/mori/playground/rust/asm_test) Finished dev [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 0.82s Running `target/debug/asm_test` 8 asm!

item.title: Decompile APK
item.description: System # Linux v5.8 Tools # apktool yay -S android-apktool dex2jar yay -S dex2jar jd-cmd yay -S jd-cmd https://github.com/kwart/jd-cli Commands # Decompress: apktool d -s -o decompiled base.apk Convert .dex files to .jar: cd decompiled dex2jar classes.dex dex2jar classes2.dex ... Decompile: jd-cmd classes-dex2jar.jar -od src jd-cmd classes2-dex2jar.jar -od src .

item.title: Installing Common Apps in Ubuntu
item.description: Applications # vim # sudo apt install vim-gtk3 emacs # Add ppa: sudo add-apt-repository ppa:kelleyk/emacs sudo apt update sudo apt upgrade If only need CUI: sudo apt install emacs26-nox GUI: sudo apt install emacs26 vscode # sudo snap install code --classic git # sudo apt install git tmux # sudo apt install tmux build essential # sudo apt install build-essential tweaks # sudo apt install gnome-tweak-tool LibreOffice # sudo add-apt-repository ppa:libreoffice/ppa sudo apt update sudo apt upgrade sudo apt install libreoffice Google Chrome # echo "deb [arch=amd64] http://dl.

item.title: Install Arch Linux with BTRFS (and Backup with Snapper)
item.description: System # CPU: Intel UEFI Boot Multi-disk support with btrfs Devices: /dev/vda /dev/vdb /dev/vdc Commands # Keymap # Change keymap: loadkeys jp106 Time # Timedatectl: timedatectl set-ntp true Mirrorlist # Optimize mirror list: pacman -Syy pacman -S reflector # `python` might be required reflector -c Japan --sort rate -a 6 --save /etc/pacman.

item.title: Usage of virsh
item.description: Commands # sudo virsh setvcpus [vm_name] [vcpu_num] --config --maximum sudo virsh setvcpus [vm_name] [vcpu_num] --config

item.title: File Encoding with nkf
item.description: Installation # sudo apt install nkf Usage # To convert any file to UTF-8 + LF: nkf -w -Lu --overwrite input_file To convert C files in current directory to UTF-8 + LF: find . -iname "*.c" -type f -print0 | xargs -0 nkf -w -LF --overwrite

item.title: Remove Kernel
item.description: How to remove kernel # Remove the following entries. /boot/vmlinuz-[version] /boot/initrd.img-[version] /boot/System.map-[version] /boot/config-[version] /lib/modules/[version]/* /var/lib/initramfs_tools/[version] Update the grub configuration. update-grub Reference # How can I remove compiled kernel https://askubuntu.com/questions/594443/how-can-i-remove-compiled-kernel Kernel/Removal https://wiki.gentoo.org/wiki/Kernel/Removal

item.title: Using Custom Style File
item.description:
Create style file (~.sty) # Create new command # 
ewcomand and enewcommand allow you to define new command. Usage: 
ewcommand{\command-name}[argument-number][default]{definition} enewcommand{\command-name}[argument-number][default]{definition} Example: 
ewcommand{\myfig}[4][width=5cm]{  egin{figure}[H] \centering \includegraphics[#1]{#2} \caption{#3} \label{fig:#4} \end{figure} } Create new environment # 
ewenvironment and enewenvironment allow you to define new environment. Usage: 
ewenvironment{environment-name}[argument-number][default]{before}{after} enewenvironment{environment-name}[argument-number][default]{before}{after} Example: \definecolor{code-bg}{rgb}{0.95, 0.95, 0.95} 
ewenvironment{code}[2][] {\VerbatimEnvironment egin{minted}[breaklines, frame=lines, bgcolor=code-bg, #1]{#2}} {\end{minted}} Add style files # Put your file under /usr/local/share/texmf/tex/latex and execute mktexlsr as a root user.

item.title: Call Systemcall in Rust
item.description: Use system-call crate # Add a dependency to Cargo.toml: [dependencies] system-call="0.1.3" Sample code: #[macro_use] extern crate syscall; fn main() { let mut pid; let cpid; let mut val = 0; unsafe { pid = syscall!(GETPID); } println!("before: val is {}, pid is {}", val, pid); unsafe { cpid = syscall!(FORK); } if cpid < 0 { println!("Fork error"); return; } else if cpid == 0 { val += 1; unsafe { pid = syscall!

item.title: Usecases of Git
item.description: Cancel git add # git reset # or git reset <file> Cancel git commit # # Reset contents git reset <tag> --hard # Reset commit and keep changes git reset <tag> --soft View previous commit # git checkout <tag>

item.title: HariboteOS with Ubuntu
item.description: Binary editor # hexedit Install: sudo apt install hexedit ghex Install: sudo apt install ghex Assembler # nasm (or gas) Usage: nasm input.asm -o output (-l output.lst) C compiler # gcc Usage: gcc -march=i486 -m32 -fno-pie -nostdlib -T [linkerfile] -g *.c *.o -o bootpack.bin Make OS image # Merge asmhead.bin and bootpack.bin: cat asmhead.bin bootpack.bin > os.

item.title: Dual Booting on Multiple Disks (Windows10 and ArchLinux)
item.description: System # Preinstalled OS: Windows 10 (64 bit Home) CPU: Intel GPU: Nvidia SSD win linux Goal # Dual booting Windows 10 and Arch Linux Arch Linux with BTRFS Commands # Keymap # Set keymaps: loadkeys jp106 Mirrorlist # Optimize mirror list: pacman -Syyy pacman -S reflector reflector -c Japan --sort rate --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist Disk Partitioning # Find windows EFI partition: fdisk -l In my case: NAME MAJ:MIN RM SIZE RO TYPE MOUNTPOINT sda 8:0 0 931.

item.title: Configurations for Gpu Passthrough
item.description: System # Arch Linux CPU: Intel Core-i9 9900K (8 cores, 16 threads) GPT: Nvidia RTX 2060 Initialization # /etc/default/grub - GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="loglevel=3 quiet" + GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="loglevel=3 quiet intel_iommu=on vfio_iommu_type1.allow_unsafe_interrupts=1 iommu=pt" /etc/modules-load.d/vfio-pci.conf + pci_stub + vfio + vfio_iommu_type1 + vfio_pci + kvm + kvm_intel Update grub.cfg: sudo grub-mkconfig -o /boot/grub/grub.cfg Reboot Isolating the GPU # Find the device ID of the GPU: lspci -nn | grep NVIDIA Output: 01:00.

item.title: MP4 Manipulation with ffmpeg
item.description: Installation # sudo apt install ffmpeg Usage # Command Description ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -crf 30 output.mp4 Compression. The higher the crf value, the higher the compression ratio. ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -an output.mp4 Mute.

item.title: Implementing Original Systemcall
item.description: System # Linux kernel 5.8.x diff # diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile index a3b3cc4d7ee2..0e202ce51444 100644 --- a/Makefile +++ b/Makefile @@ -1070,7 +1070,7 @@ export MODORDER := $(extmod-prefix)modules.order export MODULES_NSDEPS := $(extmod-prefix)modules.nsdeps ifeq ($(KBUILD_EXTMOD),) -core-y += kernel/ certs/ mm/ fs/ ipc/ security/ crypto/ block/ +core-y += kernel/ certs/ mm/ fs/ ipc/ security/ crypto/ block/ my_syscall/ vmlinux-dirs := $(patsubst %/,%,$(filter %/, \ $(core-y) $(core-m) $(drivers-y) $(drivers-m) \ diff --git a/arch/x86/entry/syscalls/syscall_64.

item.title: Using minted for Syntax highlighting
item.description: About # minted is a package that enable to add syntax highlight by using Pygments library. Installation # Pygments is written in Python and can be installed by pip pip install Pygments Package minted is already installed if you use texlive. Usage # At the preamble add: \usepackage[cache=false]{minted} \usepackage{xcolor} \definecolor{code-bg}{rgb}{0.95, 0.95, 0.95} In the document, you can use syntax highlight like this: \begin{minted}[breaklines, linenos, frame=lines, bgcolor=code-bg]{c} #include <stdio.

item.title: Connect to GitHub with SSH
item.description: Add new SSH key to github # System # Ubuntu 18.04 git version 2.17.1 xclip version 0.12 Generating SSH key # Set key name as id_rsa_github. ssh-keygen -t rsa -b 4096 Adding to ssh-agent # eval "$(ssh-agent -s)" ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa_github Adding to GitHub account # xclip -sel clip < ~/.ssh/id_rsa_github.pub Reference: GitHubにSSHで接続する

item.title: Dual Booting on Mono Disk (Windows10 and ArchLinux)
item.description: Preparation # Install Windows 10 Disk layout: Create partition for linux: Boot with iso Commands # loadkeys jp106 timedatectl set-ntp true pacman -Syy pacman -S reflector reflector -c Japan --sort rate --save /etc/pacman.d/mirrorlist cfdisk /dev/sda mkfs.btrfs /dev/sda6 mkfs.fat -F32 /dev/sda5 mount /dev/sda6 /mnt btrfs su cr /mnt/@ btrfs su cr /mnt/@home btrfs su cr /mnt/@snapshots btrfs su cr /mnt/@var_log umount /mnt mount -o noatime,compress=lzo,space_cache=v2,subvol=@ /dev/sda6 /mnt mkdir -p /mnt/{boot,home,.

item.title: Configurations for Single Root I/O Virtualization
item.description: System # Hardware CPU: Intel Core-i9 9900K NIC: Intel Corporation I350 Gigabit Network Connection Motherboard: PRIME H310M-AT R2.0 OS Host: Arch Guest VM1: ubuntu 20.04 server VM2: ubuntu 20.04 server Hardware setting # Enable VT-d on UEFI BIOS Enable SR-IOV # Add modprobe config (/etc/modprobe.d/igb.conf) options igb max_vfs=4 #blacklist igbvf Add kernel options in order to enable IOMMU by editing /etc/default/grub: -GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="loglevel=3 quiet" +GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="loglevel=3 quiet intel_iommu=on iommu=pt" Update grub config: sudo grub-mkconfig -o /boot/grub/grub.

item.title: Embedded System Development with platformio
item.description: Installation # PlatformIO is written in Python. You can install following command: pip3 install platformio Basic usage # You can use pio instead of platformio. Command Description pio boards [Query] You can see supported boards. pio init --board [ID] Initialize current directory. pio lib search [Query] Search library. pio lib show [Query] Show description of the library.

item.title: Implementing Original Hypercall
item.description: System # Linux kernel 5.8.x Host # diff --git a/arch/x86/kvm/x86.c b/arch/x86/kvm/x86.c index ba7e98a3a..9f41689b4 100644 --- a/arch/x86/kvm/x86.c +++ b/arch/x86/kvm/x86.c @@ -7696,6 +7696,30 @@ int kvm_emulate_hypercall(struct kvm_vcpu *vcpu) kvm_sched_yield(vcpu->kvm, a0); ret = 0; break; + case KVM_HC_HELLO_HYPERCALL0: + trace_printk("Hello world!\n"); + break; + case KVM_HC_HELLO_HYPERCALL1: + trace_printk("a0 = %d\n", a0); + break; default: ret = -KVM_ENOSYS; break; diff --git a/include/uapi/linux/kvm_para.h b/include/uapi/linux/kvm_para.h index 8b8660984..bceb8d1be 100644 --- a/include/uapi/linux/kvm_para.h +++ b/include/uapi/linux/kvm_para.

item.title: Cheat Sheet for Shell Script
item.description: Array # Test code: #!/bin/sh array=(0 1 2 3) echo $array echo ${array[0]} echo ${array[@]} Output: 0 0 0 1 2 if # Test code: #!/bin/sh val0=0 val1=1 # if [ $val0 = 0 ]; then echo val0 = 0 elif [ $val0 = 1 ]; then echo val0 = 1 else echo otherwise fi # AND if [ $val0 = 0 ] && [ $val1 !

item.title: LFS (Large File Storage)
item.description: About # The maximum file size that can be uploaded to github is 100MB. Git Large File Storage enables to upload large file that is larger than 100MB. System # Arch Linux Install & Setup # install: yay -S git-lfs Global configuration changes: git lfs install Use case # Current directory has two files (hello.txt is small enough and large.

item.title: Desktop Environment
item.description: Comands # Graphic driver # Graphic card: # Intel sudo pacman -S xf86-video-intel # nvidia sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms Display server # xorg: sudo pacman -S xorg Display manager # lightdm: sudo pacman -S lightdm lightdm-gtk-greeter gdm: sudo pacman -S gdm Desktop environment # xfce: sudo pacman -S xfce4 xfce4-goodies gnome: sudo pacman -S gnome gnome-tweaks budgie: sudo pacman -S budgie-desktop gnome Activate display manager # lightdm: systemctl enable lightdm gdm: systemctl enable gdm

item.title: Filmware Update with fwupd
item.description: System # OS: Ubuntu 18.04 (64bit), Arch Device: Thinkpad X1 Carbon (6th) Install # Install fwupd with: # Ubuntu sudo apt install fwupd # Arch sudo pacman -S fwupd Usage # Refresh data from server: fwupdmgr refresh Check device for updates: fwupdmgr get-updates Update and install: fwupdmgr update Tips # If the update does not apply, follow the steps below.

item.title: Install Haskell
item.description: Install Stack # Commands: paru -S stack Basic Usage # Create new projects: stack new proj-name Main source code is created in the proj-name/app as Main.hs. . ├── app │ └── Main.hs ├── ChangeLog.md ├── LICENSE ├── package.yaml ├── proj-name.cabal ├── README.md ├── Setup.hs ├── src │ └── Lib.hs ├── stack.yaml ├── stack.yaml.lock └── test └── Spec.hs Compile: stack build Run: stack exec proj-name-exe

item.title: Configurtions for BSPWM
item.description: Commands # Graphic driver # Graphic card: sudo pacman -S nvidia nvidia-utils nvidia-dkms BSPWM # Display server, window manager and etc.: sudo pacman -S xorg xorg-xinit bspwm picom dmenu sxhkd nitrogen arandr Terminal emulator and brawser: sudo pacman -S alacritty firefox Create config files: mkdir .config/bspwm mkdir .config/sxhkd cp /usr/share/doc/bspwm/examples/bspwmrc .config/bspwm/ cp /usr/share/doc/bspwm/examples/sxhkdrc .config/sxhkd/ Change terminal emulator: vim /.

item.title: Source Code Reading with find and grep
item.description: Usage # find . -type f \( -name '*.c' -o -name '*.h' \) -print0 | xargs -0 grep -C 3 -Hni 'root_hpa' find # grep #

item.title: Install Prolog
item.description: System # OS: Arch Linux Install # Commands: sudo pacman -S swi-prolog Basic Usage # Sample program: %% family.pl parent(alice). parent(bob). child(charlie). child(dave). parent_child(alice, charlie). parent_child(alice, dave). parent_child(bob, charlie). parent_child(bob, dave). sibling(X,Y) :- parent_child(Z, X), parent_child(Z, Y). Start interactive shell with swipl. $ swipl Welcome to SWI-Prolog (threaded, 64 bits, version 8.2.3) SWI-Prolog comes with ABSOLUTELY NO WARRANTY. This is free software.

item.title: Installing Common Applications in Arch Linux
item.description: Package manager # Yay git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/yay.git cd yay makepkg -si Paru git clone https://aur.archlinux.org/paru cd paru makepkg -si Shell # zsh sudo pacman -S zsh zsh-completions chsh -s $(which zsh) # or chsh -s /bin/zsh Terminal # Alacritty sudo pacman -S alacritty Xfce4 terminal sudo pacman -S xfce4-terminal Gnome terminal yay -S gnome-terminal-transparency Terminal multiplexer # tmux sudo pacman -S tmux Editor # VSCode yay -S visual-studio-code-bin vim sudo pacman -S gvim emacs sudo pacman -S emacs-nox Backup # Timeshift yay -S timeshift Browser # Brave browser yay -S brave-bin Virtual Machine Monitor # KVM

item.title: Document Generator (doxygen, sourcetrail)
item.description: Documentation Generator # Doxygen Sourcetrail

item.title: Install Curry
item.description: About PAKCS # PAKCS enables to run programs written in Curry language. Curry combines the features of functional and logical programming languages. PAKCS converts Curry code to Prolog and execute. Install on Ubuntu # At first, you have to install swi-prolog. sudo apt-add-repository ppa:swi-prolog/stable sudo apt update sudo apt install swi-prolog In Ubuntu, you can install PAKCS by following command. sudo apt install pakcs However, due to unknown errors, it is recommended to download from download site.

item.title: Power Management
item.description: Install # Install tlp: sudo pacman -S tlp Check dependencies (Thinkpad only?): tlp-stat -b Install recommended package (e.g. acpi_call). Setting # When using btrfs as a file system: sudo vim /etc/default/tlp # /etc/default/tlp SATA_LINKPWR_ON_BAT=max_performance Start # Start tlp: sudo tlp start Systemctl: sudo systemctl enable tlp

item.title: Creating Bootable USB with dd
item.description: Commands # dd bs=4M if=/path/to/archlinux.iso of=/dev/sdx status=progress && sync

item.title: Arch Build System
item.description: Install # Install asp: sudo pacman -S asp Usage (kernel build) # Create directory for build kernel: mkdir build cd build Download PKGBUILD via asp: asp update linux asp export linux Edit PKGBUILD: cd linux vim PKGBUILD 3c3 < pkgbase=linux --- > pkgbase=linux-custom 64d63 < make htmldocs 191c190 < pkgname=("$pkgbase" "$pkgbase-headers" "$pkgbase-docs") --- > pkgname=("$pkgbase" "$pkgbase-headers") Downloads source code: makepkg -so user@host linux % tree -C -L 2 .

item.title: Configurations for Spotify
item.description: HiDPI # Edit /usr/share/applications/spotify.desktop -Exec=spotify %U +Exec=spotify --force-device-scale-factor=1.5 %U Download error # ==> ERROR: Failure while downloading http://repository.spotify.com/pool/non-free/s/spotify-client/spotify-client_1.1.56.595.g2d2da0de_amd64.deb Solution: cd ~/.cache/paru/clone/spotify/ wget --continue http://repository.spotify.com/pool/non-free/s/spotify-client/spotify-client_1.1.56.595.g2d2da0de_amd64.deb mv spotify-client_1.1.56.595.g2d2da0de_amd64.deb spotify-1.1.56.595-x86_64.deb paru -Syu

item.title: Configuration of Emacs
item.description: No-window-system mode # Emacs can be run in the terminal by emacs -nw. However you got error message Fontset ‘tty’ does not exist, if you changed font for GUI. How to resolve the problem # Add the following command. (if (display-graphic-p) (progn (set-face-attribute 'default nil :family "Ricty Diminished Discord") (set-fontset-font (frame-parameter nil 'font) 'japanese-jisx0208 (cons "Ricty Diminished Discord" "iso10646-1")) (set-fontset-font (frame-parameter nil 'font) 'japanese-jisx0212 (cons "Ricty Diminished Discord" "iso10646-1")) (set-fontset-font (frame-parameter nil 'font) 'katakana-jisx0201 (cons "Ricty Diminished Discord" "iso10646-1"))) ) display-graphic-p means: This function returns t if display is a graphic display capable of displaying several frames and several different fonts at once.

item.title: Gnome configuration
item.description: test section # test
